scope.target: DEVWF-002 software-development end-to-end orchestration and acceptance contract
constraints: Activate from high-level multi-stage development intent without requiring skill names
Define test-execution as a pluggable capability without creating a new skill
Default unit and integration tests; keep specialized tests conditional
Make spec compliance unselected by default and 100 percent fail-closed after explicit activation
Commit durable validated stages or coherent batches rather than skill invocations
non_goals: Adopt an unevaluated external test skill
Force E2E or Playwright into every development workflow
Rewrite shared or pushed Git history

steps: Normalize activation and capability routing contracts
Add the pluggable test-execution capability and outcomes
Define approval pauses, commit density, and closeout evidence
Add downstream end-to-end fixtures and migration notes
execution.last_completed_step: Implemented and independently verified the deterministic preclassified activation oracle and repository-verified fresh-session acceptance fixture
execution.next_action: Completed; preserve arbitrary natural-language classification for the separately governed EVAL-001 model-in-loop boundary

results.summary: The current dev-workflow contract activates from preclassified high-level intent without skill names, pauses at approval boundaries, resolves optional target-aware test execution, treats spec compliance as selectable, and resumes from a complete repository-verified handoff without hidden conversation state.
tests_run: 15 workflow handoff tests passed and the fresh-session fixture invoked validate-workflow-handoff.py --verify-repository
scoped git diff --check passed with expected CFG-001 line-ending warnings
skill-creator quick validation passed for Agents and Claude runtime wrappers
independent transient auditor verification confirmed the honest deterministic/model boundary and complete handoff evidence
results.residual_risk: Arbitrary natural-language classification quality remains EVAL-001 model-in-loop work; environment-dependent target tests remain target-owned evidence.
